era_context: Early 6th Dynasty Egypt (if the 5th–6th Dynasty transition has occurred), Akkadian Empire dominance in Mesopotamia, mature Harappan Indus Valley Civilization. This is a period of relative stability in the major civilizations, though underlying political fragmentation is occurring in Egypt.

events.title: 6th Dynasty Egypt: Early reign of Teti or Pepi I
events.description: The 6th Dynasty is established, with Teti (c. 2345–2333 BCE) or Pepi I (c. 2333–2305 BCE) as pharaoh, depending on chronological assignment. The 6th Dynasty is characterized by the continued construction of pyramids and the increasing power of provincial governors (nomarchs). Trade expeditions are sent to Nubia, the Levant, and the Red Sea region. The administrative structure of the Old Kingdom remains intact, but centralized authority is gradually weakening.
key_figures: Teti or Pepi I (6th Dynasty pharaoh)

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from pyramid complexes and provincial sites

events.certainty_note: The 6th Dynasty is well-attested archaeologically, but absolute dating remains uncertain (±10–20 years). Assignment of this year to a specific pharaoh depends on which chronological system is used.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for this year. The historical record is blank.
historiographic_note: This year is not documented in any surviving source. The narrative is inferred from broader chronological frameworks.
